metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Relocate the DET veto for G5 to a post-processing filter on the LLM findings list in `run_pass1` (or the orchestrator) — applied after `pass1_chunk` returns, since `det_floor.py` executes before LLM-tier findings exist and cannot suppress them.
findings.finding: The plan names `det_floor.py` as the home for the DET veto that suppresses G5 'flat/undecomposed' findings, but det_floor.py executes entirely before LLM-tier findings exist — it produces DetResult objects for its own P1–P9 checks and never observes or can suppress the list returned by pass1_chunk (which runs G5 as exec:1-TURN). The veto must be a post-processing filter on the findings list, applied in run_pass1 (pass1.py) or the orchestrator after pass1_chunk returns, not inside det_floor.py.
findings.suggested_fix: In `run_pass1` (pass1.py), after collecting all findings, add a filter step: drop any finding whose `criteria` contains `'G5'` AND whose `finding` text signals a flat/undecomposed verdict AND `len(ctx.children) > 0`. This is the only point in the call graph where both the LLM finding and the store-derived child count are co-observable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the veto's exact seam: it must be a post-Pass-1 findings filter (e.g. a step after `run_pass1()` returns, before `partition_findings()`), not a `det_floor.py` check, since DET runs before LLM findings exist.
findings.finding: The DET veto placement is architecturally incompatible with the flow: det_floor.py (and the det_invariants seam) run in orchestrator step 2, before Pass-1 LLM calls in step 4. A G5 'undecomposed' finding does not exist yet when the DET floor runs, so a det_floor.py check cannot veto it. The correct seam is a post-Pass-1 filter on the findings list (e.g. inside or after run_pass1/partition_findings), but the plan does not name this seam.
findings.suggested_fix: In ## Scope / files, replace 'det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)' with the actual file + function: e.g. 'add a `_veto_g5_if_has_children(findings, ctx)` filter called in `production_batch_runner.py` immediately after `run_pass1()` returns, dropping any finding whose `criteria` includes `G5` and whose text matches a flat/undecomposed pattern, when `len(ctx.children) > 0`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the G5 'flat/undecomposed' veto must be a POST-LLM filter applied to the findings list (e.g. in `run_pass1` after the ThreadPoolExecutor results are gathered, or in the orchestrator before partition_findings), not a pre-LLM check in det_floor.py or det_invariants.py.
findings.finding: The plan names 'det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)' as the target for the G5 veto, but det_invariants.py is the dynamic project-DET phase for external detector-registry checks — it is NOT the natural seam for a built-in criterion suppression veto. Adding a G5 veto there would conflate the project-overlay detector mechanism with a core built-in suppression rule, and det_invariants.py only runs after the LLM tier has already produced findings. The correct seam is either a post-LLM filter in pass1.py/orchestrator.py (suppressing G5 findings before they are returned) or a dedicated hook in det_floor.py applied before the LLM tier runs.
findings.location: Scope / files section: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)'
findings.suggested_fix: In run_pass1 (pass1.py), after collecting findings from the LLM tier pool, add a filter step: drop any finding whose criteria includes 'G5' and whose text signals 'flat/undecomposed' when len(ctx.children) > 0. Document this as a deterministic post-LLM suppression step, distinct from the pre-LLM DET floor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the DET-veto novelty: demonstrate (e.g. with the regression test case) that injecting the authoritative child summary into G5's prompt context (AC 1) alone is *insufficient* to prevent the false-positive before committing to a cross-tier suppressor inside det_floor.py; if it is sufficient, drop the veto and rely on AC 1 + the existing G5 anti-FP checklist.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel cross-tier DET veto that suppresses LLM-tier G5 findings post-generation, but does not justify this novelty against the simpler established alternative of relying on the authoritative context injection (AC 1) combined with G5's existing anti-FP rubric checklist items — which already instruct the model not to flag tickets that have children.
findings.location: Scope / files — det_floor.py / AC line: 'A DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding when the ticket has >=1 child'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a regression test for a ticket that has >=1 child but whose children have poor altitude/content: verify G5 still emits a content/altitude finding (i.e., the veto is narrow and does not over-suppress all G5 findings).
findings.finding: The test plan has no specified test for the case where a ticket has >=1 child but G5 SHOULD still fire for child-content or altitude reasons — validating that the DET veto does not over-suppress. AC line 2 asserts 'G5 still judges child-content/altitude' as a constraint, but no regression test covers this boundary.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `tests/unit/` case: construct a `ctx` with `children=[...]` where children exist but represent poor decomposition quality (e.g., implementation-level tasks in a strategy epic), run the G5 finder + DET veto, and assert that a G5 altitude/content finding is still emitted — confirming the veto only suppresses the 'flat/undecomposed' subtype.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g. `pytest tests/unit/test_g5_decomp_det.py -v` for AC2+AC3, and a `grep` or log-capture assertion for AC1's injected context block) so done-ness is verifiable without reading source.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given for any of the three acceptance criteria; the plan describes what to build but provides no pytest invocation, log assertion, or deterministic command that produces durable evidence of success.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to ACs: 'Proving: `pytest tests/unit/test_g5_decomp_det.py -v` must show two passing cases (with-children veto, without-children pass-through). For AC1, add an assertion in the same test that the prompt string passed to the G5 finder contains the literal substring "DECOMPOSITION STATE (from store):".'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact injection mechanism for the DECOMPOSITION STATE block: either (a) append it to the whole chunk's instructions (and note that co-chunked criteria will see it), or (b) move G5 to a dedicated single-criterion chunk when children are present, or (c) inject it into the plan_review_G5.md prompt template via a new template variable.
findings.finding: The context injection mechanism is unspecified for a multi-criterion chunk: G5 has facet 'scope-intent' and is chunked with other same-facet criteria (confirmed in criteria_routing.json:247). `pass1_chunk` builds a single `instructions` string for ALL criteria in the chunk via `_criterion_block(c)`. The plan says to inject a 'DECOMPOSITION STATE' block 'into the G5 finder context,' but the prompt assembly has no per-criterion injection hook — injecting into the chunk instructions would expose the block to all co-chunked criteria, and there is no mechanism to inject it only when G5 is in the chunk.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Scope/files test description to explicitly cover BOTH regression cases: (a) epic-with-children suppresses G5, and (b) childless monolithic ticket still fires G5 — matching AC3's two-sided requirement.
findings.finding: The Scope/files section describes only ONE regression test scenario ('regression test for the previously-misfired case'), but AC3 mandates TWO distinct test scenarios: (a) an epic with children must NOT yield a G5 undecomposed finding, AND (b) a genuinely monolithic childless ticket still MUST yield one. The positive/true-positive test case has no covering description in the Scope section, creating both an orphan AC element (E1) and a cross-section narrowing contradiction (COH).
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope line to: 'regression tests for (a) the previously-misfired case — epic with children no longer yields a G5 undecomposed finding — and (b) a genuinely monolithic childless ticket still yields a G5 finding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` (specifically `pass1_chunk`, where the `instructions` payload for G5 is assembled at line 292-296) to the Scope / files list as the primary edit target for the DECOMPOSITION STATE context injection.
findings.finding: `pass1.py` is named as the injection site for the DECOMPOSITION STATE block, but the actual prompt/instructions construction for the G5 finder chunk occurs in `passes.py:pass1_chunk` (line 292-296). `pass1.py:run_pass1` passes a bare `ctx.plan_text` string to `_pass1_with_ladder`, which passes it unchanged to `passes.pass1_chunk`; there is no per-criterion context-injection hook at the `pass1.py` level. `passes.py` is an unenumerated (and arguably the primary) edit target for the context-injection AC.
findings.location: Scope / files — `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py` (injection AC); `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:pass1_chunk` (actual prompt assembly at line 292-296)
findings.suggested_fix: In `passes.py:pass1_chunk`, when the chunk contains a G5 criterion, append the child summary block to the `instructions` string before constructing the `RunRequest`. The summary can be passed in as an optional `extra_context: str | None = None` parameter, populated by the caller in `pass1.py` from `ctx.children`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact child dict keys used to render `[alias - title (status)]` in the DECOMPOSITION STATE block (e.g. `ticket_id` for alias, `title` for title, `state.get('status')` for status) and specify the fallback when a key is absent.
findings.finding: The format string `[alias - title (status)]` in the proposed DECOMPOSITION STATE block references a 'status' field on child dicts, but the plan does not name which key holds this value. Children are assembled via `_reads.show_ticket(cid)` (orchestrator.py:185), and the resulting dict's status key is not documented in the plan. The field could be `state['status']`, a top-level `status`, or require a lookup from the child's `state` subdict — leaving the rendered string vocabulary undefined for the implementer.
findings.location: ## Scope / files — pass1.py bullet (the format string `[alias - title (status)]`)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `[alias - title (status)]` in the plan text with an explicit example: `[{c['ticket_id']} - {c.get('title', '?')} ({c.get('status') or c.get('state', {}).get('status', 'unknown')})]` and confirm the field path against the show_ticket return shape.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (and document in the plan) that all downstream consumers of det_floor.py / G5 findings — orchestrator, report formatters, any callers that act on finding counts — are either unaffected by the new veto or updated accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan changes the behavior of the G5 finder (DET veto now suppresses findings that previously reached callers) but does not acknowledge whether downstream consumers of det_floor.py / the G5 findings list — such as the orchestrator, report formatter, or any caller that currently acts on a G5 'undecomposed' result — depend on the old behavior and need updating or are verified to be unaffected.
findings.location: Scope / files — det_floor.py entry; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note in Scope / files: 'No downstream consumer of det_floor.py output acts specifically on G5 findings in a way that breaks under suppression (verified by grep/search for G5 handling post-floor), OR list any consumer that must be updated.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the preferred insertion point for the DET veto (e.g. 'add to `det_floor.py` at the existing per-criterion post-processing loop; if that module does not exist, create it adjacent to `pass1.py`') so the executor has a concrete default without investigation.
findings.finding: The location of the DET guard is given as 'det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)', leaving the executor to discover or decide where the veto belongs without a stated default.
findings.location: Scope / files — second bullet: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the bullet to: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py — add a post-processing veto in the existing per-criterion filter loop (create the module if absent, placed adjacent to pass1.py).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the 'store-derived deterministic veto' pattern, the chosen seam location (det_floor.py or equivalent), and the rationale so future contributors understand which findings can be suppressed by store data and why.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — a deterministic store-data veto that overrides LLM-generated G5 findings — but does not include any documentation update (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc) capturing this new pipeline contract or the rationale for where the veto lives (det_floor.py vs. 'the DET-invariant seam').
findings.location: Scope / files — det_floor.py bullet; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a CLAUDE.md or docs/adr/NNNN-det-veto-pattern.md entry stating: 'G5 (and potentially other finders) may be deterministically suppressed by store-derived facts in det_floor.py. A finding is dropped when the store unambiguously contradicts the LLM's premise (e.g., ctx.children > 0 contradicts "undecomposed"). New veto rules must be added here, not inside individual finders.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
